creator: sunpoet@FreeBSD.org
text:
This is not the issue about "USE_PYTHON=concurrent".

Please follow the steps in UPDATING entry 20210425 to rebuild dependent packages.

When you change the default python, you have to rebuild old port (lang/cython@py38 here) to release the room (/usr/local/bin/cygdb here) for new one (lang/cython@py310 here).
The details are as follows:

1. (DEFAULT_VERSIONS=python=3.8)
When python38 is the default, lang/cython (=lang/cython@py38) will install both /usr/local/bin/cygdb-3.8 and /usr/local/bin/cygdb.
Note that /usr/local/bin/cygdb is a symbolic link to /usr/local/bin/cygdb-3.8 and is only installed when the flavor matches the default python flavor/version.

2. (DEFAULT_VERSIONS=python=3.10)
After you changed the default python to python310, you have to rebuild py38-flavored ports in order to remove /usr/local/bin/cygdb which is needed for lang/cython@py310 (/usr/local/bin/cygdb-3.10).
During the rebuild, since flavor (@py38) does not match the default python flavor/version (@py310), lang/cython@py38 only installs /usr/local/bin/cygdb-3.8.

3. (DEFAULT_VERSIONS=python=3.10)
When you install lang/cython (=lang/cython@py310), it installs /usr/local/bin/cygdb (a symbolic link to /usr/local/bin/cygdb-3.10) because the flavor matches the default python flavor/version (@py310).

It works as intended.
